concerns.text: Tasks T030a and T030b explicitly instruct the implementation to 'report Posterior Probability... and Bayes Factors instead of p-values'. This directly violates spec.md SC-003 which mandates 'report exact p-value'. The task deliverable falls short of the spec's acceptance criterion, constituting a semantic weakening of the requirement.

Tasks T029a and T029c describe generating data for '≤80%' and '≥20%' of conditions respectively without specifying that these percentages must sum to 100% across the *same* set of solvents. The current phrasing allows for generating data for 100% implicit and 100% explicit independently, violating the 'integrated' requirement of spec.md FR-005 which implies a mixed approach for the total dataset.

Task T015 defines the 'Primary Data Generation' as synthetic data with 'no inherent correlation' to test the null hypothesis. However, spec.md US-1 and FR-002 require the system to 'initiate the experimental protocol' and 'capture transient-absorption data' (implying real measurement). Relying entirely on synthetic data for the primary dataset without a clear distinction that this is a CI-only placeholder (vs. the actual research data) weakens the FR-002 requirement for real-call testing.

Task T035 requires implementing a registry to define a specific instrument model (e.g., 'Edinburgh Instruments LP-series'). Spec.md FR-002 and Assumptions only mandate the *capability* (ns–μs resolution) and do not authorize a specific vendor model. Hard-coding a specific hardware dependency in the tasks violates the spec's agnostic stance and introduces an unverified constraint not present in the FRs.
